dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: `schemas/blocker-taxonomy.yaml`, `schemas/blocker-taxonomy.schema.json`, `internal/cli/blocker_envelope.go`, `internal/cli/blocker_envelope_test.go`, and the doc citation all exist at the required paths, the D1/D2 codes emitted in `internal/cli/validate_definition.go` and `internal/cli/validate_ownership.go` are all registered, bounded-scope files `row_workflow.go`/`row_semantics.go` were untouched, and `go test ./...` passed.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: `internal/cli/blocker_envelope_test.go` exercises taxonomy load, all initial code resolution, unknown-code panic, interpolation success, and missing-placeholder panic, covering each new D3 code path with at least one test.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: The implementation matches the specified interface contract: `LoadTaxonomy()` parses the canonical YAML, `EmitBlocker(code string, interp map[string]string)` returns the D1/D2 JSON envelope, the schema uses `additionalProperties:false` at every object level, and the code regex now accepts kebab-case or snake_case as required.

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: `git show --name-only` for `5510f4ee` includes files outside D3 ownership, but the commit message explicitly documents and justifies each non-D3 change (D2/D4/D5/D6), so the spillover is recorded rather than undocumented.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: The Go files are `gofmt`-clean (`gofmt -l` returned no output), the code follows project conventions (`fmt.Errorf` wrapping, clear structs/tests), and the repo test suite stays green with `go test ./...`.
